fleet_narrative: Yesterday's market spiked sharply to $61.4/MWh fleet average with all 32 nodes trading above $100/MWh RT, signaling acute supply tightness despite moderate 50.4 GW load and 33 GW combined wind+solar generation. This universal spike across the entire portfolio creates exceptional discharge opportunities, but the extreme DART compression in West Texas (−36.77) and elevated coastal spreads (−20.31) indicate DA expectations were severely underpriced, requiring cautious positioning today.
trend_analysis: Over five days, ERCOT has transitioned from renewable-saturated conditions (April 16: 55.1 GW wind+solar, negative DART) to constraint-driven scarcity (April 20: 33 GW renewable, all nodes spiking). West Texas DART has deteriorated 47 points in four days (10.18 → −36.77), reflecting either transmission congestion or localized generation loss; North Texas shows more stability but yesterday's −11.25 is materially negative, suggesting morning DA bids significantly overestimated resource availability or underestimated evening ramp demands.

anomalies.detail: DART averaged −49.43 with 10.55 intraday spread, indicating DA pricing was off by nearly $50/MWh; investigate transmission constraint or outage in West Texas that materialized post-DA.
Only top-5 DART performer with −9.39 (least negative), suggesting North Texas DA was relatively well-calibrated while others in region missed by 11+ points; monitor for local supply advantage.
Hit >$100/MWh RT despite North Texas' historical resilience; indicates demand or transmission dynamics overrode regional generation adequacy.
Co-located storage facility spiked to >$100 RT with −35.95 DART, suggesting either unit constraint or that local solar underperformed relative to DA forecast.
Spiked >$100 RT yet intraday spread only 4.51, indicating sharp sustained scarcity rather than volatile swings; likely structural West Texas congestion.

nodes_to_watch.reason: If West Texas congestion persists today, this node's extreme −49 DART represents the largest value capture opportunity; monitor real-time constraints and DA bids for reversal signals.
Despite being a top DART performer (−11.52), it still spiked >$100 RT; today's positioning here will determine if North Texas can replicate profitable discharge or if tightness has become structural.
Unusual spike in typically stable North Texas market suggests demand or transmission dynamics shifting; if persistent, this node offers high discharge value but increased execution risk.

market_conditions: ERCOT experienced a sharp supply-demand imbalance on 2026-04-20 with sub-35 GW renewable output, resulting in fleet-wide price spikes exceeding $100/MWh at all 32 HEN nodes despite moderate 50.4 GW peak load.
charging_rationale: Universal sub-$100 spike nodes and positive RT spreads across all regions create strong discharge optionality today; charge aggressively if DA pricing remains materially above yesterday's RT baseline, particularly targeting West Texas nodes where DART compression suggests DA will again underprice evening scarcity.
